selection.topic: How to manage and dealing with fake content on social media.

I enjoy seeing the world through the eyes of others—new recipes, garden tips, or stories from faraway places. But with that comes a growing sense of unease when I see images or claims that seem too good to be true, like miracle cures or fake testimonials. My curiosity leads me to want to understand, but my high level of neuroticism makes me worry deeply when I see something that doesn’t align with what I know to be true. I’ve learned to pause before sharing anything, to ask myself if it makes sense, if it’s supported by facts, or if it’s just emotional manipulation. It’s not easy, especially when I spend so much time online, but I believe that being mindful, asking questions, and staying grounded in reality is the best way forward. response_parsed.topic: How to manage and dealing with fake content on social media.

persona.time_onsocialmedia: You spend several hours daily on social media

response_parsed.opinion: As a retired woman who has spent many years in Bologna, I find social media both enriching and troubling. I enjoy seeing the world through the eyes of others—new recipes, garden tips, or stories from faraway places. But with that comes a growing sense of unease when I see images or claims that seem too good to be true, like miracle cures or fake testimonials. My curiosity leads me to want to understand, but my high level of neuroticism makes me worry deeply when I see something that doesn’t align with what I know to be true. I’ve learned to pause before sharing anything, to ask myself if it makes sense, if it’s supported by facts, or if it’s just emotional manipulation. I often compare what I see with what I’ve learned from my Christian values—truth, integrity, and compassion. I’ve started using trusted sources, like official health sites or verified news outlets, to cross-check claims. I also talk to my grandchildren, who are more tech-savvy, and they help me spot red flags. It’s not easy, especially when I spend so much time online, but I believe that being mindful, asking questions, and staying grounded in reality is the best way forward. In the end, I try to protect not just my own peace of mind, but also the well-being of those around me who may be more vulnerable to deception.
response_parsed.reasoning_summary: The persona is a 79-year-old retired woman with a high openness to new experiences and a strong emotional sensitivity, which increases her vulnerability to misinformation. Her curiosity drives her to engage with content, but her high neuroticism makes her prone to anxiety when faced with uncertainty. Her religious beliefs provide a moral framework for discerning truth. Given her daily social media use, she needs practical, grounded strategies to filter content. The solution combines personal values, critical thinking, and intergenerational support. The advice is tailored to her life in Bologna, her hobbies, and her emotional profile, emphasizing reflection, verification, and community trust.
response_parsed.tone: reflective, cautious, grounded, with a touch of personal concern and moral clarity
citations: World Health Organization, 2023 - Mental health and digital media
